text: I'm the world's leading almost therapist
and Gary and Leslie are commercial
pilots who are stranded in Florida due
to a snowstorm and they need therapy and
I am here. So Gary, what's the issue?
Leslie, what's the issue?
>> Well, I mean, you can't control the
weather. I mean, you know, you get
storms and stuff and people want to fly
and it's just not safe.
>> Yeah, but do you not know that the storm
is going to come? Why don't you plan a 2
a.m. flight so we can all get out and
get home? Well, we got to sleep a little
bit just every now and then because we
don't want to hear you complaining the
whole time because we're falling asleep
up front.
>> You do fall asleep up front.
>> No, we try to stay awake.
>> Listen, you do understand why people are
upset, right? People want to get home.
They want to get home.
>> Get off. They get upset, but we try to
remain calm. All pilots have these.
>> We think they're they're to communicate,
but they're really to block out all the
people complaining.
>> Wait, so you're not even listening to
the people complaining? We try not to
listen to the people complain.
>> I have a question for you. Airplanes
also have wheels on them, right? So, if
you can't fly, why don't you just start
driving on the highway?
>> Take down all of the trees and the power
lines.
>> As long as I'm seated in first class. My
bags are checked in. I don't care. Just
get it to move. Just break some trees on
the way. There's a lot of environmental
people. They will replant them. Is it
hard to fly an airplane?
>> You're only going one way. There's no
reverse. They push you out.
>> So, what do you guys do? You're not
pushing. You're not pulling. You're just
pressing a button.
>> Push the button.
>> And you get paid to do that.
>> You know what? You're pushing all our
buttons waiting over there. That's what
you're pushing. [laughter]
